scope.summary: Implement generate_avatar_pose MCP tool: accepts a text keyword description or reference image artifact_id, calls a vision-language LlmClient to produce a PoseSpec (joint angles in degrees), applies it to a tailor_avatars body proxy, and emits TailorAvatarPoseGenerated
acceptance_criteria: PoseSpec struct is defined with joint_angles: HashMap<String, f32> (degrees, keyed by joint name matching the IK skeleton used in tailor_body_proxies) and a natural_description: Option<String>
generate_avatar_pose MCP tool accepts keyword: Option<String> and reference_image_artifact_id: Option<String>; at least one must be non-null
Tool constructs a CompletionRequest with the keyword and/or image and prompts the LLM to emit a PoseSpec JSON; the response is validated against the joint name set of the target avatar
The resulting PoseSpec is stored in tailor_avatar_poses (new dated migration: YYYY_MM_DD_tailor_avatar_poses.sql) and linked to the tailor_avatars row via a FK
The active pose is applied to the body proxy (updates tailor_body_proxies capsule/sphere geometry) before the next simulate_garment call
TailorAvatarPoseGenerated KernelEventType variant is emitted on success (wire string TAILOR_AVATAR_POSE_GENERATED)
ModelManual is updated with generate_avatar_pose tool entry and MANUAL_VERSION is bumped

The tool is local-inference-first: the LlmClient implementation used MUST NOT require a cloud API; graceful fallback to a set of 12 built-in named poses (standing, A-pose, T-pose, walking-mid, etc.) when the LLM call fails
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::avatar::pose_generation_from_keyword (keyword 'standing with arms slightly out' produces a PoseSpec with plausible shoulder joint angles)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::avatar::pose_builtin_fallback (LlmClient mock fails -> built-in A-pose returned)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::generate_avatar_pose_schema (inputSchema auto-generated by schemars includes keyword and reference_image_artifact_id)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::storage::pose_persisted_and_linked (TailorAvatarPoseGenerated event in EventLedger; tailor_avatar_poses row present)
scope.risk_if_missed: Models and operators have no way to set a meaningful avatar pose for fit evaluation or turntable capture without hardcoding one of the built-in poses. Garments are always evaluated in A-pose or T-pose, which does not represent real wear tension. Fit heatmaps (AutoFit group) are meaningless if the evaluation pose does not match the intended use. MD's AI Pose Generator is specifically designed to solve this; without parity, automated fit workflows are limited to static evaluation.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
